summary: Zhenan Bao discusses her long-term vision of creating electronic skin that not only mimics human skin but enhances human functionality with new sensing capabilities. She reflects on how science fiction inspires real-world technological advancements and emphasizes the importance of interdisciplinary collaboration, diversity, and mentorship in research, drawing from her foundational experiences at Bell Labs.

principles.statement: Artificial skin should transcend human biological limitations.
principles.rationale: Because electronic skin is artificially constructed, it presents an opportunity to integrate superhuman functionalities, such as sensing chemicals or distant heat, rather than just replacing lost function.
principles.source_quote: since we're building this artificial um uh sheet or electronic sheet then uh it gives us the possibility to to add additional functionalities that our human scheme may not have

principles.statement: Science fiction serves as a blueprint for future technological functions.
principles.rationale: Fictional scenarios inspire scientists to develop technologies with similar capabilities, which are then adapted for practical, everyday uses rather than superhero feats.
principles.source_quote: those type of scenario that we see in science fiction they some form of it eventually become realized in our everyday life... they Inspire us to think about developing technology that can have the same function but then we find better usage

principles.statement: Breakthrough problem-solving requires diverse, interdisciplinary collaboration.
principles.rationale: Bringing together people from different backgrounds, disciplines, and genders creates an environment that fundamentally shapes how complex problems are approached and solved.
principles.source_quote: I think it's about the collaboration between people from different disciplines things the diversity of people coming from different background and gender

mental_models.name: Beyond-Biomimicry
mental_models.description: A lens for engineering where mimicking biology is only the baseline. Once the artificial version is built, it serves as a platform to integrate capabilities that biology naturally lacks.
mental_models.example: Creating electronic skin that feels and looks human, but can also sense chemical environments or detect heat from far away without physical contact.

heuristics: Build diverse research groups across different disciplines and backgrounds to shape how problems are approached.
Use science fiction to inspire technological functions, but optimize them for practical everyday usage.

signature_quotes.text: our long-term vision is uh to to mimic human skin uh and uh in terms of building a electronic sheet that uh mimics the sensing functionality of human skin and uh looks just like human skin and feels like human skin
signature_quotes.context: Describing the core mission of her research group.

signature_quotes.text: since we're building this artificial um uh sheet or electronic sheet then uh it gives us the possibility to to add additional functionalities that our human scheme may not have
signature_quotes.context: Explaining how artificial skin can surpass biological skin.

signature_quotes.text: those type of scenario that we see in science fiction uh they some form of it eventually become uh realized in our everyday life
signature_quotes.context: Discussing the relationship between science fiction and technological development.
